title: 控方证人
summary:
Following a discussion regarding the "gift of the blind"—the idea that children possess a perception of reality that adults lose—Jeffrey falls seriously ill with a long-standing lung disease. As his condition worsens, Lady Lancaster hears the heartbreaking sobs of a child within the house. While in a state of unconsciousness, Jeffrey speaks to a "little boy," expressing a desire to help him leave. Upon briefly waking, Jeffrey whispers, "Okay, I'll be right there," before falling back into a coma. Subsequently, Lady Lancaster and Mr. Wimborne experience a frightening supernatural event involving the sounds of triumphant laughter and the footsteps of two invisible children departing the house.

In a separate narrative, Dr. Menner advises Mrs. Hart that her heart function is weak and that she must avoid exertion and mental worry to survive. He recommends the installation of an elevator. Mrs. Hart’s nephew, Charles Richewe, also proposes installing a radio to alleviate her boredom. Despite Mrs. Hart’s skepticism and fear that radio waves and electricity will disturb her, Charles uses his technical expertise to persuade her. An elevator and a radio are subsequently installed. While Mrs. Hart finds the radio to be an ugly, buzzing device, she shows interest as Charles tunes into broadcasts from Berlin and Brussels. It is noted that Mrs. Hart’s niece, Miriam Hart, had previously lived with her but returned to her mother's house after marrying a man her aunt disapproved of.
